title: No canonical block-api id-allocation helper — orchestrator scripts re-implement next-id allocation
description: block-api exposes read/write primitives but NO id-allocation helper (no nextId/allocateId; verified absent across block-api.ts). file-block-item.ts re-implements id allocation inline (file-block-item.ts:156-174: parse prefix+width from the id pattern, scan existing items, increment) as a parallel path. Any other surface needing auto-id (the in-pi append tools, future CLIs) must re-implement or hand-scan. The missing primitive is the allocation-side analog of the validation divergence in FGAP-082: allocation logic lives in the CLI, not the library, so it can't be shared + diverges (e.g. it doesn't dereference $ref item schemas — FGAP-083).

evidence.reference: no exported id-allocation function (only read/write primitives)
inline re-implementation of next-id allocation (prefix/width parse + scan + increment) — the logic that belongs in a shared block-api helper

impact: id-allocation is duplicated/divergent and not reusable by the in-pi tool surface (the Pi append tools have no auto-id at all). Extracting a canonical allocateId(cwd, block) helper would let file-block-item, the in-pi tools, and future surfaces share one correct implementation (and fix FGAP-083 once).
proposed_resolution: Add a block-api id-allocation primitive (e.g. allocateId(cwd, blockName) → next id from the block-kind's resolved id pattern + existing items, dereferencing $ref item schemas) and route file-block-item + any in-pi auto-id through it. DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface: library primitive + CLI + (optionally) Pi tool. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 — commit 5ecd7bd / TASK-060 / VER-032: canonical block-api nextId(cwd,block) helper; file-block-item + the in-pi append-block-item autoId twin route through it; inline re-implementation removed.]
